name: Sant Bahadur Singh (Jalandhar)
body: The information below was obtained from the magazine ‘Weekly Simran’ Basti Sheikh, Jalandhar.

Very few fortunate souls are blessed with musical knowledge as well as spiritual enlightenment. For this reason, Bhai Sahib Sant Bahadur Singh Ji was fondly known as both ‘Bhai Sahib’ and ‘Sant’ by the Sikh Sangat. Born in 1920 at Khanewal, now in Pakistan, their family moved to Jalandhar after the partition in 1947. Sant Bahadur Singh Ji’s interest in music developed since he was a child and he started doing Kirtan from very young. His parents had ‘Parkash’ of Sri Guru Granth Sahib Ji at home and the entire family gathered every morning to sing Asa Ki Vaar. This was their daily Nitnem. Before forming his own ‘jatha’, Sant Bahadur Singh Ji accompanied Sant Sujan Singh Ji on the side harmonium, and received many blessings as a result of being in Sant Sujan Singh Ji’s company.

Sant Bahadur Singh Ji’s wife, Bibi Beant Kaur Ji played a very important role in his life. She used to take care of their home while he was traveling abroad to spread the sublime message of Gurbani through his Kirtan. Whenever the Sangat visited their home to meet Sant Bahadur Singh Ji or to book programs, she would greet them and take care of all their needs. Sant Bahadur Singh Ji and Bibi Beant Kaur Ji had seven children; four daughters and three sons. Their daughters include Joginder Kaur Ji, Gurmeet Kaur Ji, Harkiran Kaur Ji and Harprit Kaur Ji. Their three sons, Gurmukh Singh Ji, Jeet Singh Ji and Hari Singh Ji were all talented musicians. Jeet Singh Ji, the youngest son, worked as an officer in Punjab Sindh Bank and passed away in a road traffic accident at a young age. Bhai Gurmukh Singh Ji possessed a sweet voice that complemented the voice of Sant Bahadur Singh Ji and he used to provide accompaniment on the ‘tanpura’. He settled in New York many years ago but visited Jalandhar annually to conduct prayers in memory of Sant Bahadur Singh Ji. Bhai Gurmukh Singh Ji too has since passed, and his son, Ridaypreet (Prince) is still based in New York. Jeet Singh Ji’s son, Ajiz Jetha Singh is currently based in Jalandhar. Their two daughters-in-law, Inderjit Kaur and Kanwaljit Kaur continued to instill Sikh values in their children. Currently, Gurmeet Kaur Ji is the oldest living member of the family, followed by Hari Singh Ji.

Having performed Kirtan for over 50 years, Sant Bahadur Singh Ji had several members in his jatha at various times. Among them were Dalip Singh Ji, Swaran Singh Ji and Ujagar Singh Ji. Many famous Kirtanis learned from Sant Bahadur Singh Ji and credit their success to his blessings. Sant Bahadur Singh Ji traveled to many countries including the United States, Canada, Africa, Singapore, Thailand, Malaysia, United Kingdom and others. Santa Bahadur Singh Ji left for his eternal abode in 1985 at the age of 65.
If anyone has more recordings or photos of Sant Bahadur Singh Ji, please contact us.
